channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania's Central Bank Focuses on Economic Stability and Digital Inclusion
item.description: The Bank of Tanzania (BoT) is prioritizing price stability, financial sector resilience, and digital financial inclusion in its five-year strategy to support Tanzania's Vision 2050 economic transforma...

item.title: India Monitors Bangladesh-China Teesta River Project Developments
item.description: India has expressed cautious monitoring of Bangladesh's plans to develop the Teesta River with Chinese assistance, emphasizing its focus on regional developments. During Bangladesh Prime Minister Tari...

item.title: Elections Alberta Rejects Corb Lund's 'Water Not Coal' Petition
item.description: Elections Alberta has rejected musician Corb Lund's 'Water Not Coal' petition, which sought to ban new coal mining in the Eastern Slopes of the Rocky Mountains. The petition initially met the required...

item.title: Prior threats surface in Ottawa case involving double child homicide, arson and suicide
item.description: Police in Ottawa are investigating a tragic case involving the deaths of two young boys, aged seven and twelve, who were allegedly killed by their father, Mohammed Al-Lami, in their south Ottawa home....

item.title: Kamba Musician Solo Sita Dies After Illness at Kenyatta Hospital
item.description: Renowned Kamba Benga musician Solo Sita (Bonface Kyambu) has passed away at Kenyatta National Hospital after a prolonged illness. The news, confirmed by close friend DJ Biado, has sent shockwaves thro...

item.title: Canada's Nuclear Energy Strategy Faces Criticism for Industry Benefits and Safety Concerns
item.description: The article critiques Canada's nuclear energy strategy, highlighting concerns that it prioritizes industry profits over public safety and environmental protection. Critics argue the policy benefits nu...

item.title: President Samia emphasizes innovation to boost DITF's global status during 50th anniversary
item.description: Tanzania’s President Samia Suluhu Hassan has urged stakeholders to prioritize innovation as the Dar es Salaam International Trade Fair (DITF), known as Sabasaba, celebrates its 50th anniversary. Speak...

item.title: DSS Prosecutes Sowore for Cyberstalking and Defamation, Calls for Stricter Cybercrime Laws
item.description: The Department of State Services (DSS) is prosecuting activist Omoyele Sowore for alleged cyberstalking and criminal defamation over social media posts labeling President Tinubu as a 'criminal.' Direc...

item.title: US Backs Pakistan's Right to Defend Against Terrorists After Military Operations
item.description: The US State Department has expressed support for Pakistan's right to defend itself against terrorist attacks following coordinated military operations by Pakistani security forces targeting militant ...

item.title: Royal Commission on Antisemitism Reveals Deepening Crisis
item.description: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion has uncovered alarming evidence of systemic harassment and intimidation faced by the Jewish community, with hearings revealing a surge in hate ...

item.title: Drones Used to Enforce Firework Regulations During Independence Day Celebrations
item.description: The article discusses the increasing use of drones by law enforcement agencies in California to monitor and enforce firework regulations during Independence Day celebrations. As traditional methods of...

item.title: Andy Warhol's Uncle Sam: A Photographic Portrait
item.description: The article discusses Andy Warhol's 1981 photographic series depicting Uncle Sam, commissioned by gallerist Ronald Feldman. James A. Mahoney, an actor known for advertising work, was hired to portray ...

item.title: Extreme Heat Forces Closure of Trump's State Fair
item.description: The Great American State Fair on the National Mall was forced to close early due to a record-breaking heatwave that gripped Washington, D.C., with temperatures nearing 100°F and heat indexes exceeding...

item.title: TANROADS Commits to Enhancing Road Infrastructure and Public Service Standards

item.description: Australia's Socceroos squad, led by coach Tony Popovic, faces a crucial World Cup knockout match against Egypt in Dallas Stadium. The game is pivotal as the team seeks to become the first Australian s...

item.title: Democrats Prepare Investigations Into Trump's Financial Disclosures and Inner Circle
item.description: The article discusses Democrats preparing a hostile audit of President Donald Trump and his inner circle, aiming to investigate his financial disclosures and potential conflicts of interest. Trump's 2...

item.title: Blind boxes and dopamine: Understanding their impact on children
item.description: Blind boxes, which contain mystery toys, are not classified as gambling under Australian law. While they don't meet the legal criteria for gambling—such as the possibility of losing money—they share n...

item.title: Kenyan Creator Reveals Cost of Hair Transplant Procedure
item.description: Kenyan content creator Tileh Pacbro has shared the cost of his hair transplant procedure, revealing that he spent KSh 230,000 to restore his hairline through a procedure involving approximately 1,800 ...

item.title: The Onion's Satirical Take on Infowars and Its Legal Battle with Alex Jones
item.description: The Onion, a satirical news organization, has launched a parody of Alex Jones' Infowars platform, aiming to highlight the legal and ethical controversies surrounding the conspiracy theorist. The parod...

item.title: Pete Hoekstra's Claims About Canada Funding the Gordie Howe Bridge Spark Controversy
